mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in UNRATE signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months when UNRATE increases month-over-month, or Rank IC > 0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.5%, it signals an aggressive easing cycle that compresses the risk-free rate and equity discount factor, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as liquidity effects transmit to asset prices.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and increases in the unemployment rate capture a coordinated supply-demand contraction that amplifies recession risk beyond either signal in isolation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of INDPRO decline and UNRATE increase fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 5.5%), month-over-month contractions in retail sales (RSAFS) indicate an acceleration of consumer demand collapse that is not yet reflected in equity prices, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, RSAFS contractions are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not significantly lower than the unconditional Sharpe.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in housing starts signal weakening residential investment and durable goods demand, reducing expected corporate earnings in construction and related sectors and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate exceeding 50 basis points signals an aggressive central bank response to acute financial stress, triggering a short-term liquidity-driven relief rally in the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines > 0.50pp, 5-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a historically low federal funds rate and a rapidly rising unemployment rate captures a liquidity-trap recession where conventional monetary easing loses efficacy, causing prolonged negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS level and UNRATE change does not significantly predict negative 60-day returns (p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms the single-factor UNRATE model in out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the time-series correlation between INDPRO changes and 60-day forward returns is positive and significant.
